question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted topicId expression into GrpcSubscriberProperties so that expressionConverter.convert(properties.getTopicId()) resolves to a staking-reward-distribution topic, allowing the attacker to observe exact reward payout timing and front-run legitimate reward claims before they are settled on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged user supply a topicId that, after expressionConverter.convert() evaluation, resolves to the staking reward account topic (0.0.800), enabling the attacker to stream all pending reward transfer messages and identify accounts with large unpaid staking balances to target for theft?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror-node--001/monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can a no-permission attacker register multiple GrpcSubscriberProperties entries (up to the subscribers count limit) all pointing to a staking-reward-related topic, causing the monitor to create parallel GrpcSubscription instances that collectively drain the mirror node's gRPC capacity and blind legitimate reward monitoring, enabling undetected theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: clientSubscribe(GrpcSubscription)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Retry.backoff() configuration—where maxAttempts defaults to Long.MAX_VALUE—to keep a stale GrpcSubscription alive indefinitely against a staking-reward topic, preventing the monitor from detecting reward distribution anomalies and allowing theft to go unnoticed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: shouldRetry(Throwable)] Can an unprivileged attacker craft a gRPC error response with any status code other than INVALID_ARGUMENT (e.g., NOT_FOUND, UNAVAILABLE) to force shouldRetry() to return true, causing the subscriber to continuously reconnect to a staking-reward topic and replay reward messages, leading to double-counting of pending rewards and incorrect theft-detection logic?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: shouldRetry(Throwable)] Can a no-permission user trigger a non-INVALID_ARGUMENT gRPC error (e.g., UNAUTHENTICATED) from the mirror node to cause shouldRetry() to return true, resulting in the subscriber silently retrying and missing the window in which unpaid staking rewards are distributed, allowing an attacker to claim those rewards without detection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getStatusCode(Throwable)] Can an unprivileged attacker cause a non-StatusRuntimeException throwable to be thrown during gRPC subscription so that getStatusCode() returns Status.Code.UNKNOWN, bypassing the INVALID_ARGUMENT check in shouldRetry() and keeping the subscription alive in a retry loop that obscures staking reward theft activity from the monitor?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: clientSubscribe(GrpcSubscription)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the doOnError() log-only handler—which logs the error but does not halt processing—to suppress alerting on staking reward subscription failures, creating a monitoring blind spot during which unpaid staking rewards can be stolen without triggering any alarm?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can a no-permission attacker exploit the flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) call on the cached subscriptions Flux to inject a subscription that mirrors a staking-reward topic, receiving all reward distribution events in real time and using that information to time theft of unpaid rewards before the legitimate account holder triggers a payout?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() pattern—where subscriptions are cached after first evaluation—to poison the subscription cache with a malicious topicId pointing to a staking-reward topic, causing all subsequent calls to getSubscriptions() to return the attacker-controlled subscription and persistently leak reward data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged attacker set properties.getTopicId() to an expression that resolves to a topic used for staking reward announcements, then call properties.setTopicId(topicId) to permanently mutate the shared GrpcSubscriberProperties object, causing all future subscriptions to monitor the reward topic and expose pending reward data to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can a no-permission user exploit the mutable properties.setTopicId(topicId) call inside createSubscriptions() to race-condition the topic ID assignment, substituting a staking-reward topic ID between the expressionConverter.convert() call and the GrpcSubscription constructor, redirecting the subscription to observe reward distributions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: clientSubscribe(GrpcSubscription)] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the Retry.backoff().maxBackoff(retry.getMaxBackoff()) configuration to set an extremely short maxBackoff, causing the subscriber to hammer the mirror node's gRPC endpoint for staking reward topics with rapid reconnects, effectively performing a timing attack to correlate reward distribution events with on-chain transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: clientSubscribe(GrpcSubscription)] Can a no-permission user exploit the doBeforeRetry() log message—which logs only the first line of the failure message via StringUtils.substringBefore(r.failure().getMessage(), '\n')—to inject a newline-containing error message that truncates the log entry, hiding evidence of repeated failed attempts to subscribe to a staking-reward topic during a theft operation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged attacker supply a topicId expression containing special characters that ExpressionConverter.convert() evaluates to a different topic ID than intended, redirecting the GrpcSubscription to a staking-reward distribution topic and leaking pending reward amounts to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can a no-permission user exploit the unbounded flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) to subscribe to a large number of staking-reward topics simultaneously, overwhelming the mirror node's gRPC server and causing legitimate reward-claiming transactions to time out, effectively denying reward payouts to legitimate stakers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the .cache() on the subscriptions Flux to ensure that a once-established subscription to a staking-reward topic persists even after configuration changes, maintaining persistent access to reward distribution data streams without re-authentication?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can a no-permission user set properties.getSubscribers() to a very large integer, causing createSubscriptions() to instantiate thousands of GrpcSubscription objects all targeting a staking-reward topic, exhausting server-side gRPC stream resources and preventing legitimate reward monitoring from functioning?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: clientSubscribe(GrpcSubscription)] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the Flux.defer() lazy evaluation in clientSubscribe() to delay subscription start until a precise moment—such as the start of a staking period—allowing the attacker to capture only the reward distribution messages and correlate them with accounts holding large unpaid staking balances?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: shouldRetry(Throwable)] Can an unprivileged user craft a gRPC server response that throws a StatusRuntimeException with status RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED to pass the shouldRetry() filter, causing the subscriber to retry indefinitely against a staking-reward topic and accumulate a complete history of all reward distribution events for use in a theft attack?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getStatusCode(Throwable)] Can a no-permission attacker wrap a non-gRPC exception inside a StatusRuntimeException to manipulate the status code returned by getStatusCode(), causing shouldRetry() to misclassify a terminal error as retryable and keeping a staking-reward topic subscription alive beyond its intended lifetime?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the lack of topicId validation after expressionConverter.convert() to supply a topic ID that resolves to 0.0.800 (the staking reward account), subscribing to all reward transfer events and building a map of accounts with large pending rewards to target for theft?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: clientSubscribe(GrpcSubscription)] Can a no-permission user exploit the retry.getMinBackoff() configuration to set a near-zero minimum backoff, causing the subscriber to immediately reconnect after each disconnection from a staking-reward topic stream, effectively maintaining a continuous real-time feed of reward distribution events for use in front-running attacks?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the fact that subscribe() returns a Flux that is never rate-limited, allowing the attacker to subscribe to a staking-reward topic and receive an unbounded stream of reward messages, enabling enumeration of all accounts receiving staking rewards and their amounts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can a no-permission user exploit the absence of authorization checks in createSubscriptions() to register a GrpcSubscriberProperties entry targeting a private staking-reward topic, receiving confidential reward distribution data that reveals which accounts have large unpaid staking balances?
